hook_text: I almost hired an HR consultant for OMR 2,000/month. Then I built the software instead.
body:
Three years ago, I was running my first tech project in Oman.

Payroll was a mess. I'd already paid two late-payment fines.

A consultant quoted me OMR 2,000/month to handle HR compliance.

I was ready to sign.

Then I spent one weekend mapping every rule myself:
— WPS deadlines
— End-of-service calculations
— Leave accrual under Labour Law
— Sanad registration windows
— Gratuity triggers

I filled a notebook.

And I realized: this isn't complicated. It's just uncodified.

Nobody had written it down in a way that software could enforce.

So I did.

Two years of conversations with HR directors, MOL inspectors, accountants.

SmartPRO Hub is that notebook — turned into a system.

The OMR 2,000/month consultant? We still talk. He now refers clients to us.

---

Every founder has a moment where they almost outsourced the thing they should have built.

What was yours?
rejected_reason: pre-PR#12 backlog purge — queue hygiene 2026-07-15 (regenerate via corrected pipeline)
